title: NOC 33109 – Other Assisting Occupations in Support of Health Services | GTR Immigration
description: Other Assisting Occupations in Support of Health Services under NOC 33109 include a wide range of healthcare support professionals working in hospitals, clinics, laboratories, and rehabilitation settings across Canada. As healthcare systems expand and diagnostic and therapeutic services grow, this TEER 3 occupation continues to demonstrate strong national demand. For internationally trained candidates, NOC 33109 may qualify under Express Entry and selected Provincial Nominee Programs, providing a structured pathway toward Canada PR.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category3 – Health occupationsTEER3 – Occupations usually require college certificate or on-the-job trainingMajor Group33 – Assisting occupations in support of health servicesSub-Major Group331 – Assisting occupations in support of health servicesMinor Group3310 – Assisting occupations in support of health servicesNOC Code33109

duties: Administer hearing tests under audiologist supervision
Apply and adjust casts, splints, and traction equipment
employment_requirements: Specialized audiometry training for Audiometric Technicians
Completion of an orthopedic technologist program (for Orthopedic Technologists)
Registration with the Canadian Society of Orthopaedic Technologists (may be required by employers)
Certification by the Joint Commission on Allied Health Personnel in Ophthalmology for Ophthalmic Assistants (may be required)
Completion of a 6–9 month sterile processing program for Sterile Processing Technicians
International applicants may require employer-recognized certification depending on province.
salary_prospects: Annual earnings typically range from $32,800 to $65,200, depending on specialization and province.
job_outlook: Growth in outpatient and specialized care facilities
Rising demand for sterile processing and laboratory support
Candidates with specialized technical training may experience stronger employment opportunities.
labour_market_demand: Professionals under NOC 33109 are employed in:
Hospitals
Medical clinics
Nursing homes
Offices of healthcare professionals
Optical retail stores and laboratories
Pathology laboratories
Rehabilitation facilities
Between 2022–2031, projections indicate:
44,300 new job openings
41,900 new job seekers

This steady employment outlook is supported by expanding healthcare infrastructure and increased demand for specialized technical support roles, particularly in provinces such as Ontario, British Columbia, Alberta, Quebec, Saskatchewan, and Manitoba.
Why Immigrate to Canada Under NOC 33109?
Professionals in this unit group benefit from:
TEER 3 eligibility under Express Entry (program dependent)
Broad occupational coverage across multiple healthcare environments
Opportunities in public and private healthcare sectors
Career progression into specialized technical roles
Stable employment in essential healthcare services
As hospitals and diagnostic centres expand, assisting occupations remain vital to operational efficiency.
pathways: If you are qualified under NOC 33109, you may be eligible for immigration pathways to Canada through Express Entry or Provincial Nominee Programs.
Contact GTR Immigration today to begin your pathway toward permanent residence in Canada under NOC 33109.
